utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format includes 5 items: (1) health_coverage, (2) property_coverage, (3) travel_coverage, (4) additional_services, and (5) total_budget. Now check the final plan: it contains all five items. 1) health_coverage: includes both physical and mental, benefits, limits, deductible, and monthly cost — fully specified. 2) property_coverage: includes home/contents and related aspects, but does not include explicit costs (only notes that monthly cost is included), still it mentions cost inclusion, which is acceptable as cost handling. 3) travel_coverage: includes types, benefits, and cost status (included), so acceptable. 4) additional_services: lists enhancement services with cost status implied (included). 5) total_budget: includes full monthly and annual costs with currency. Therefore, all 5 required items appear and are sufficiently specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The AI assistant’s final coverage plan includes three main categories: health_coverage (both physical and mental), property_coverage, and travel_coverage. The health section features premium-level features like global access, low deductible (5%), and within-week specialist appointments, which aligns most closely with the 'PrimeCare Premium Plan' from the ground truth (rating 6). The property coverage includes home/contents, electronics, liability, temporary accommodation, and natural disasters — this matches the comprehensive profile of 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' (rating 9). The travel_coverage includes international and adventure travel benefits such as medical evacuation, trip cancellation, and adventure sports coverage, matching best with 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' (rating 10). Additional services like concierge and telemedicine are add-ons, corresponding to 'Telemedicine Services' (rating 8) and 'Concierge Services' (rating 2) from the ground-truth ratings.
